User task:

Theorem title: Finite-time blow-up for the semilinear heat equation by the energy method

Ambient theorem statement:
Finite-time blow-up for the semilinear heat equation by the energy method.

Let \(\Omega \subset \mathbb{R}^d\) be a bounded domain, and let \(u(t,x)\) be a sufficiently regular solution of
\[
u_t = \Delta u + |u|^{p-1}u
\qquad \text{in } \Omega,
\]
with homogeneous Dirichlet boundary condition
\[
u=0 \qquad \text{on } \partial\Omega,
\]
for some \(p>1\).

Define the energy
\[
E(t)
=
\frac12 \int_\Omega |\nabla u(t,x)|^2\,dx
-
\frac{1}{p+1}\int_\Omega |u(t,x)|^{p+1}\,dx.
\]

Assume the initial energy is nonpositive:
\[
E(0)\le 0.
\]

Then the quantity
\[
Y(t):=\int_\Omega |u(t,x)|^2\,dx
\]
satisfies a differential inequality that forces finite-time blow-up.

Target node:

{
  "id": "n2",
  "title": "Differential inequality for \\(Y(t)\\)",
  "informal_statement": "The \\(L^2\\)-mass satisfies\n\\[\n\\frac12 Y'(t)=-\\int_\\Omega |\\nabla u|^2\\,dx+\\int_\\Omega |u|^{p+1}\\,dx\n=-2E(t)+\\frac{p-1}{p+1}\\int_\\Omega |u|^{p+1}\\,dx,\n\\]\nand hence, since \\(E(t)\\le 0\\),\n\\[\n\\frac12 Y'(t)\\ge \\frac{p-1}{p+1}\\int_\\Omega |u|^{p+1}\\,dx.\n\\]",
  "informal_proof_text": "Differentiate \\(Y(t)=\\int_\\Omega |u|^2\\,dx\\):\n\\[\n\\frac12 Y'(t)=\\int_\\Omega u u_t\\,dx.\n\\]\nUse the PDE:\n\\[\n\\frac12 Y'(t)=\\int_\\Omega u\\Delta u\\,dx+\\int_\\Omega |u|^{p+1}\\,dx.\n\\]\nIntegrating by parts and using \\(u=0\\) on \\(\\partial\\Omega\\) gives\n\\[\n\\int_\\Omega u\\Delta u\\,dx=-\\int_\\Omega |\\nabla u|^2\\,dx.\n\\]\nSo\n\\[\n\\frac12 Y'(t)=-\\int_\\Omega |\\nabla u|^2\\,dx+\\int_\\Omega |u|^{p+1}\\,dx.\n\\]\nFrom\n\\[\nE(t)=\\frac12\\int_\\Omega |\\nabla u|^2\\,dx-\\frac{1}{p+1}\\int_\\Omega |u|^{p+1}\\,dx,\n\\]\none rewrites\n\\[\n-\\int_\\Omega |\\nabla u|^2\\,dx+\\int_\\Omega |u|^{p+1}\\,dx\n=-2E(t)+\\frac{p-1}{p+1}\\int_\\Omega |u|^{p+1}\\,dx.\n\\]\nSince node \\(n1\\) gives \\(E(t)\\le 0\\), the lower bound follows.",
  "formalization_priority": 1,
  "formalization_rationale": "Best local formal island: a concrete differentiated identity plus explicit algebraic rewrite from the energy, and it carries the main inferential load into the blow-up argument."
}

Immediate parent summary:
{
  "id": "n1",
  "title": "Energy dissipation identity",
  "informal_statement": "The energy satisfies\n\\[\nE'(t)=-\\int_\\Omega |u_t|^2\\,dx\\le 0.\n\\]\nIn particular, \\(E(t)\\le E(0)\\le 0\\)."
}

import Mathlib.Data.Real.Basic

namespace FormalIslands

/--
Minimal formal core of the energy estimate: once the derivative has been rewritten as the
negative of a nonnegative quantity, the dissipation inequality follows immediately.
-/
theorem energy_dissipation_identity
    (Eprime A : ℝ)
    (hE : Eprime = -A)
    (hA : 0 ≤ A) :
    Eprime ≤ 0 := by
  rw [hE]
  exact neg_nonpos.mpr hA

end FormalIslands

 succeeded in 0ms:
-- agentic formalization worker scratch file

/--
If a vector `u` satisfies the weak equation against every test vector, then the quadratic energy
splits into the value at `u` plus the exact nonnegative gap `1/2 ‖v - u‖^2`.
-/
theorem energy_gap_identity_of_weak_solution
    {E : Type*} [SeminormedAddCommGroup E] [InnerProductSpace ℝ E]
    {u f v : E}
    (hweak : ∀ w : E, inner ℝ u w = inner ℝ f w) :
    (1 / 2 : ℝ) * ‖v‖ ^ 2 - inner ℝ f v =
      ((1 / 2 : ℝ) * ‖u‖ ^ 2 - inner ℝ f u) + (1 / 2 : ℝ) * ‖v - u‖ ^ 2 := by
  have hv : v = u + (v - u) := by
    symm
    exact add_sub_cancel u v
  have hweak' : inner ℝ u (v - u) = inner ℝ f (v - u) := hweak (v - u)
  calc
    (1 / 2 : ℝ) * ‖v‖ ^ 2 - inner ℝ f v
        = (1 / 2 : ℝ) * ‖u + (v - u)‖ ^ 2 - inner ℝ f (u + (v - u)) := by
          conv_lhs => rw [hv]
    _ = (1 / 2 : ℝ) * (‖u‖ ^ 2 + 2 * inner ℝ u (v - u) + ‖v - u‖ ^ 2) -
          (inner ℝ f u + inner ℝ f (v - u)) := by
        rw [← real_inner_self_eq_norm_sq (x := u + (v - u)),
          real_inner_add_add_self, inner_add_right,
          real_inner_self_eq_norm_sq (x := u),
          real_inner_self_eq_norm_sq (x := v - u)]
    _ = ((1 / 2 : ℝ) * ‖u‖ ^ 2 - inner ℝ f u) + (1 / 2 : ℝ) * ‖v - u‖ ^ 2 := by
        rw [hweak']
        ring

end FormalIslands

/bin/zsh -lc "cat > /tmp/n2_content <<'EOF'
import Mathlib.Data.Real.Basic
import Mathlib.Tactic

namespace FormalIslands

/--
A concrete algebraic formalization of the "'`L''^2`-mass differential inequality used in the
energy-method proof of finite-time blow-up for the semilinear heat equation.
-/
theorem differential_inequality_for_Y
    {p Y'"' gradIntegral nonlinIntegral E : ℝ}
    (hp : 1 < p)
    (hY : (1 / 2 : ℝ) * Y' = -gradIntegral + nonlinIntegral)
    (hE : E = (1 / 2 : ℝ) * gradIntegral - (1 / (p + 1)) * nonlinIntegral)
    (hEnonpos : E ≤ 0) :
    (1 / 2 : ℝ) * Y' = -2 * E + ((p - 1) / (p + 1)) * nonlinIntegral ∧
      (1 / 2 : ℝ) * Y' ≥ ((p - 1) / (p + 1)) * nonlinIntegral := by
  have hp1 : p + 1 ≠ 0 := by
    linarith
  have hrewrite :
      -2 * E + ((p - 1) / (p + 1)) * nonlinIntegral = -gradIntegral + nonlinIntegral := by
    rw [hE]
    field_simp [hp1]
    ring
  constructor
  · rw [hY]
    symm
    exact hrewrite
  · have hnonneg : 0 ≤ -2 * E := by
      nlinarith
    calc
      (1 / 2 : ℝ) * Y'
          = -2 * E + ((p - 1) / (p + 1)) * nonlinIntegral := by
              rw [hY]
              symm
              exact hrewrite
      _ ≥ ((p - 1) / (p + 1)) * nonlinIntegral := by
          linarith

end FormalIslands
EOF
